title: США намерены закрыть свои дата-центры для китайских телеком-компаний
description: Федеральная комиссия по связи США (FCC) намерена запретить трем крупным китайским телекоммуникационным компаниям China Mobile, China Telecom и China Unicom использовать свои центры обработки данных на территории страны, сообщает Reuters. Также комиссия рассматривает возможность запретить американским и другим операторам связи, работающим в США, подключаться к их мощностям.

title: Djibouti election expected to extend incumbent’s 27 years in presidential office

While critics dismiss the election as “token competition”, supporters say the 78-year-old incumbent has brought stability to the Horn of Africa country strategically situated between the Red Sea and the Gulf of Aden.

title: Tehran residents welcome ceasefire but voice concerns
description: Residents in the Iranian capital Tehran have welcomed the ceasefire agreement reached with the United States, but also voiced concerns over how long it might hold. After some 40 days of deadly clashes, the United States and Iran on Wednesday agreed to a two-week ceasefire, and committed to begin a new round of peace consultations in Islamabad, the capital of Pakistan, on Friday.

title: Netanyahu faces mounting criticism as Israel’s war goals in Iran remain unmet
description: For Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu's critics, Israel is seen as the biggest loser of the conflict with Iran. His war aims including regime change and an end to uranium enrichment have not yet been achieved. And international opinion of Israel has fallen further since the start of the conflict, with a majority of Americans now having a negative view of the country.

title: Chart shows Iran may have put sea mines in Strait of Hormuz
description: Semiofficial news agencies in Iran published a chart Thursday suggesting the country’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard put sea mines into the Strait of Hormuz during the war, in a message that may be intended to pressure the US as uncertainty hangs over a days-old two-week ceasefire and further negotiations are expected in Pakistan. The charts were released by the ISNA news agency, as well as Tasnim, which is believed to be close to the Guard.

title: France's Sarkozy says no Libyan money went into winning 2007 campaign
description: France's ex-president Nicolas Sarkozy on Tuesday told an appeal trial there was "not a single cent of Libyan money" in the campaign that saw him elected in 2007. The 71-year-old, who has always denied any wrongdoing, last year became modern France's first former president to have gone to jail over the case. FRANCE 24's Antonia Kerrigan reports.

Israel said its battle against Hezbollah was not part of the US-Iran truce agreed late Tuesday.

Also: a French magazine officialises far-right leader Jordan Bardella's rumoured relationship with an Italian socialite.

title: Eritrea’s AFCON place in doubt after players disappear in South Africa
description: Seven of the 10 players that travelled from Eritrea didnt return home after their 4-1 win over Eswatini on March 31.

title: Ukraine’s veterans’ theatre turns war wounds into catharsis
description: Wounded soldiers, wives and widows workshop ideas and stage plays in Kyiv, bringing the audience to tears.

title: Thousands of Palestinians pray at Al-Aqsa Mosque after Israel’s 40-day ban
description: Around 3,000 worshippers held morning prayers at Al-Aqsa after Israel lifted restrictions.
